title: 'Doubted child’s paternity': Man kills father, wife over suspected affair
content: A shocking incident unfolded in Bulandshahr where a 28-year-old man allegedly murdered his father and wife, driven by suspicions of an illicit affair. The man reportedly used his father's licensed pistol after a heated argument. Police have arrested the accused, who claimed to have found incriminating evidence of the alleged relationship.

title: The heat that hurts, the rain that heals: Inside India’s monsoon machine
content: Delhi's intense summer heat, pushing past 45°C, is not just a discomfort but a vital part of a natural engine. This brutal heat drives the monsoon, aids soil health, ripens fruits, and suppresses pests, playing a crucial role in India's agricultural and ecological cycles.

title: 'Not in my control': Patidar on IPL final moving from Bengaluru to Ahmedabad
content: Defending champions Royal Challengers Bengaluru will face Gujarat Titans in Qualifier 1 for a direct spot in the IPL 2026 final, which will be held in Ahmedabad. RCB captain Rajat Patidar acknowledged the challenging conditions for bowlers this season, with even 200-plus scores not guaranteeing safety. He emphasized the team's focus on execution and staying calm under pressure.

title: Ranveer Singh breaks silence after film workers’ body issues directive over ‘Don 3’ exit
content: FWICE issued a non-cooperation directive against Ranveer Singh over his exit from 'Don 3', after he failed to respond to three formal notices. Ranveer's spokesperson said he chose to maintain silence with dignity. FWICE President BN Tiwari declared that no one, not even a superstar, is above industry rules.

title: CEO Mark Zuckerberg's goodbye memo to 8,000 fired Meta staff has 2 promises for survivors
content: Mark Zuckerberg's goodbye memo to 8,000 fired Meta employees on May 20 doubled as a message to the 70,000 staff still standing. The Meta CEO made two promises: no further company-wide layoffs in 2026, and better communication after weeks of internal anxiety. The memo lands as Meta funnels $125-145 billion into AI infrastructure, shifts 7,000 staff to AI projects, and cancels 6,000 open roles.

title: 'Detect, delete and deport': Bengal govt opens first illegal migrant 'holding centre'
content: West Bengal's BJP government has launched its 'detect, delete and deport' policy, opening a holding center in Malda for illegal foreign nationals. Nine suspected Bangladeshi nationals are currently housed there, awaiting verification and legal procedures. This initiative aims to streamline the deportation process for apprehended individuals, as mandated by a new national law.

title: ‘If he knew history ...’: Iran mocks Marco Rubio's Taj Mahal visit
content: Iran's Hyderabad Consulate criticized US Secretary of State Marco Rubio's Taj Mahal visit, asserting Iranian architects built the monument while the US threatens Iranian civilization. This follows Rubio's India trip and comes after Iran rejected his energy market claims. The consulate's remarks also alluded to past US threats during regional conflicts.

title: Army makes its artillery arm more lethal by giving its guns automatic aiming capability
content: The Indian Army is enhancing its 105mm field guns with automatic aiming technology, a significant modernization step. This computer-driven system drastically reduces manual effort, improving accuracy, response times, and survivability for artillery crews. This upgrade aligns with the broader Field Artillery Rationalisation Plan, aiming for a more potent and efficient artillery force.

title: Jeff Bezos to Amazon users: 'We can’t run Amazon the way NYC runs its school system'
content: Amazon founder Jeff Bezos has sharply criticized New York City's public school spending, likening its inefficiencies to a poorly run business. He argued that substantial funds aren't reaching teachers, despite high per-student costs. Bezos also dismissed 'tax-the-rich' policies, advocating for identifying and solving root causes of economic struggles instead.

title: Kerala: 24-year-old woman dies after consuming acid over 'dowry' harassment; husband in custody
content: A 24-year-old woman tragically died after allegedly consuming acid at her husband's home in Kasaragod. Fathimath Sufaida's family claims she faced harassment over dowry. An altercation preceded the incident, with her husband reportedly preventing her departure and destroying her phone. Police have taken the husband into custody.

title: Buying a home in NCR: How much it costs in Delhi, Gurugram and Noida
content: The National Capital Region is witnessing a dramatic spike in property prices. With the impending Jewar airport, Noida's real estate landscape is evolving into a high-end hub, where luxury properties are fetching premium rates. Meanwhile, Gurugram's realty scene is booming as revised circle rates elevate property values, particularly in emerging neighbourhoods. Meanwhile, Delhi is about to revise its circle rates for the first time since 2014.

title: Elon Musk says: This 'one-word' in social media profile is sure sign of a pompous retard
content: Elon Musk criticized individuals using "PhD" in their social media names, deeming it a sign of arrogance. This statement followed a debate initiated by Musk's tweet about eliminating the "researcher" title at xAI. The exchange involved AI startup CEOs, with Musk labeling "PhD" users as "pompous retards."

title: Cockroach Janta Party founder moves Delhi HC after X account blocked
content: Cockroach Janta Party founder Abhijeet Dipke has approached the Delhi High Court challenging the Centre's blocking of the group's social media accounts. The satirical movement, which gained viral traction discussing unemployment and exam leaks, saw its X account suspended on national security grounds. Dipke also alleged hacking of other accounts.

title: Is this the best time to buy a home? Expert shares why property might be more affordable in 2026
content: Homebuyers in 2026 face a complex affordability landscape. While loan rates are historically low, rising construction costs and a shrinking supply of affordable homes present challenges. Buyers must honestly assess their finances and consider long-term implications for a sustainable purchase.

title: What's an Encyclical? Five letters from Popes that left a global impact
content: Pope Leo XIV unveiled his encyclical, “Magnifica Humanitas,” addressing human dignity in the AI era. This historic document, presented with an AI pioneer, marks the Catholic Church's continued engagement with contemporary challenges through its ancient tradition of papal letters. The encyclical joins a lineage of influential papal teachings on critical social issues.

title: Martin St Louis shares Lane Hutson injury update after Canadiens scar
content: Lane Hutson avoided a serious injury scare after an awkward overtime collision with Carolina Hurricanes forward Taylor Hall. Montreal Canadiens coach Martin St. Louis provided a positive update, saying the young defenseman is on track to keep playing. Hutson struggled to skate off the ice and showed visible frustration after the painful sequence, causing concern across the NHL news landscape.

title: NFL trade rumors: Cowboys eye Dolphins’ $10m linebacker in shake-up
content: NFL trade rumors continue linking the Dallas Cowboys with Jordyn Brooks as Dallas searches for linebacker depth before the 2026 season. Brooks, currently with the Miami Dolphins, is viewed as expendable after Miami drafted Jacob Rodriguez. The veteran linebacker posted 183 tackles in 2025 and could help stabilize Dallas’ defense while giving the Cowboys more flexibility for future roster planning.

title: 'I lost my soul': Shailene Woodley opens up after Aaron Rodgers split
content: Aaron Rodgers, the Pittsburgh Steelers’ star player, has often spoken about how happy he has been in his life ever since he got married to his wife even as his ex girlfriend Shailene Woodley has often spoken about how their break up caused her depression. While Aaron Rodgers’ wife’s identity has not been revealed yet, his ex girlfriend, popular actress Shailene Woodley’s comments have sparked chaos.

title: UP: 12-year-old girl sold by mother for Rs 16,000, 10 saris, raped by ‘buyer’, auto driver
content: A 12-year-old girl from Bihar was allegedly sold by her mother for Rs 16,000 and 10 saris to a 40-year-old man in Uttar Pradesh. After a sham temple marriage, she was repeatedly raped and later abandoned, only to be preyed upon by an auto driver before her rescue. The mother, the man, and the auto driver have been arrested.

title: Diljit Dosanjh’s Ludhiana home gets bomb threat, police deployed
content: Days after singer Diljit Dosanjh's manager Gurpartap Singh Kang's house was allegedly attacked in Haryana, a bomb threat email targeting Diljit's Ludhiana residence was sent to the Ludhiana Municipal Corporation. The email, allegedly from the 'Khalistan National Party', also threatened the Mayor's residence. Police have been deployed at the singer's home.

title: Baby dreams, dark deals: The illegal IVF network spreading across India
content: A disturbing illegal fertility trade is unfolding across India, involving exploited women, unlicensed clinics, and alleged medical malpractice. Investigations reveal a vast network engaged in unlawful egg donation, surrogate exploitation, and possible human trafficking, with vulnerable women repeatedly used and financial irregularities surfacing.

title: 'Under observation': RCB sweating on Phil Salt call ahead of Qualifier 1 vs GT
content: Salt had missed the latter part of the league stage because of injury after featuring in the first six matches of the season. Speaking at the pre-match press conference on Monday, Patidar said the England batter remains under observation.

title: 17 killed as speeding minibus crashes into parked bus in Pak’s Khyber Pakhtunkhwa
content: A tragic road accident in Pakistan's Khyber Pakhtunkhwa province claimed at least 17 lives and injured five others. A speeding minibus collided with a stationary bus on the Swat Expressway near Mardan. Preliminary reports suggest driver negligence as the cause. Rescue teams responded swiftly, transporting the injured and deceased to a hospital. Investigations are ongoing into this devastating incident.

title: From 'fear of four' to 'haunted homes': Unique realty rules across the world
content: Discover fascinating property rules worldwide! Uganda mandates spousal consent for family land sales, while Germany restricts house colours to maintain neighbourhood harmony. Hong Kong's "haunted" homes sell at a discount due to past tragedies. In China, the number four is widely avoided in buildings due to its association with death, impacting floor and room numbering.

title: Saudi Arabia cashes in as Iran war blocks vital Hormuz strait
content: Saudi Arabia is capitalizing on the Iran war's disruption of the Strait of Hormuz to advance its goals as a regional energy and logistics hub. The kingdom's existing infrastructure and strategic Red Sea ports are facilitating continued oil exports and absorbing increased trade, boosting its revenue and accelerating development plans for a crucial East-West trade nexus.

title: 'It's got to be a battle': GT camp fires warning to RCB
content: Defending champions Royal Challengers Bengaluru face Gujarat Titans in IPL 2026 Qualifier 1. Both teams, finishing with equal points, boast strong batting and bowling units. GT assistant coach Vijay Dahiya highlighted the importance of bowlers and key players like Kohli, Gill, and Sudharsan in this high-stakes encounter.

title: Stalin attacks TVK after 3 AIADMK MLAs quit; EPS alleges ‘pre-planned conspiracy’
content: Tamil Nadu's political landscape is shaken as three AIADMK MLAs resigned, reportedly joining the ruling TVK party. Both DMK and AIADMK leaders have accused the Vijay-led government of "horse-trading" and orchestrating defections within the Secretariat. These resignations deepen the AIADMK's internal rifts and necessitate by-elections.

title: Pole vaulters carry equipment on e-rickshaw after breaking national record - Watch
content: Hours after becoming joint national record holders, both athletes were seen travelling in an e-rickshaw with their poles and equipment loaded alongside them after leaving Birsa Munda Stadium.

title: Man hurls petrol bomb at woman’s house in Coimbatore after she rejects his proposal
content: A woman in Coimbatore was targeted with petrol bombs at her home after rejecting the advances of a man she met on Instagram. The man, identified as Karthi from Chennai, allegedly retaliated with a group of friends after the woman cut off contact upon discovering his criminal background. Police are investigating the incident.

title: 'Dark transit': How Gulf producers keep energy shipments moving despite Hormuz closure
content: Amidst Middle East conflict, UAE and Qatar's oil giants are employing "dark transits," switching off transponders in the Strait of Hormuz to maintain energy exports. Adnoc and Qatar have adapted with controlled fleets and discreet navigation, including shuttle runs and ship-to-ship transfers, to ensure cargo movements despite geopolitical risks and limited broadcast data.

title: 'Share traits of destruction': Kharge on PM Modi and Trump's 'mutual appreciation'
content: Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge criticized Prime Minister Narendra Modi and former US President Donald Trump, stating they share a similarity in "destruction" which fuels their mutual praise. This comes after Trump expressed his "love" for PM Modi and India during a call to the US ambassador in New Delhi.

title: Padma Awards: Uday Kotak, Harmanpreet Kaur among recipients; full list of awardees
content: President Droupadi Murmu presented 66 Padma awards at Rashtrapati Bhavan. Violinist N Rajam received Padma Vibhushan. Actor Dharmendra was honored posthumously with Padma Vibhushan. Actor Prosenjit Chatterjee received Padma Shri. The ceremony recognized distinguished service in various fields. A total of 131 Padma awards were approved for 2026.

title: Hundreds of tourists stranded mid-air after technical snag hits cable car in Gulmarg
content: Hundreds of tourists faced an unexpected halt at Gulmarg's famous Gondola cable car service due to a technical glitch on Monday.  Officials confirmed that both phases of the popular attraction were suspended.  Trained rescue teams from SDRF and NDRF safely evacuated all stranded visitors from the cabins. Restoration efforts are currently in progress.

title: 'Trump is a big fan of India, PM Modi': Rubio pushes back against anti-India rhetoric
content: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio refuted claims of rising anti-India rhetoric in the United States, stating President Trump is a "big fan" of India and Prime Minister Modi. Rubio dismissed systemic hostility, emphasising Washington's commitment to strengthening ties and downplaying online "crazy stuff" as unrepresentative.

title: Dharmendra honoured posthumously: Hema Malini accepts Padma Vibhushan; Ahana breaks down
content: In a poignant moment at the prestigious awards ceremony, Hema Malini graciously accepted the Padma Vibhushan on behalf of her beloved late husband, Dharmendra, honoring his remarkable six-decade journey in cinema. The celebration also highlighted the talents of Satish Shah, who received a Padma Shri, and the melodious Alka Yagnik, awarded the Padma Bhushan.

title: Akash missile system, radars & more: Why Armenia has turned to India for defence
content: Armenia is bolstering its defence with India, seeking robust military hardware and training to counter threats from Azerbaijan, which is supported by Pakistan and Turkey. Recent high-level visits and significant arms deals, including Akash missile systems, highlight New Delhi's growing role as Armenia's key strategic partner in the South Caucasus.

title: Watch: Police visit TMC leader Abhishek Banerjee's Kolkata residence
content: Kolkata Police visited Trinamool Congress MP Abhishek Banerjee's residence, citing a routine exercise and removing security equipment. This follows notices from the Kolkata Municipal Corporation regarding alleged unauthorized construction at properties linked to Banerjee, including his official home. Banerjee has accused the BJP of political vendetta over the civic action.

title: 'He's human': Steyn makes big statement on Bumrah's poor IPL season
content: Despite a disappointing IPL 2026 campaign where Jasprit Bumrah took only four wickets, former South Africa pacer Dale Steyn backed the Indian speedster to return strongly next year and potentially win the Purple Cap. Steyn highlighted Bumrah's crucial role in India's recent T20 World Cup triumph, emphasizing his human side after a tough IPL season.

title: K'taka CM change soon? Decision may be taken in 2-3 days - Report
content: A Karnataka leadership change decision is imminent within days following crucial Congress meetings. Chief Minister Siddaramaiah met with party leaders amid ongoing speculation about a potential shift. Discussions also covered Rajya Sabha and MLC polls, and a minor cabinet reshuffle has already occurred, with portfolios being reassigned.

title: Ranveer Singh banned: FWICE issues directive over 'Don 3' dispute with Farhan Akhtar
content: FWICE has issued a non-cooperation directive against Ranveer Singh following his sudden exit from Farhan Akhtar's 'Don 3'. The complaint was filed through IFTDA, with Excel Entertainment reportedly seeking Rs 40 crore in compensation. The Producers Guild of India may also intervene to resolve the dispute.

title: CPI(M) sees silver lining in Falta Assembly re-poll results
content: CPI(M) candidate Sambhu Nath Kurmi received 40,625 votes securing 19.34 % of total votes polled; CPI(M) state secretary Md. Salim remarked that the Trinamool Congress is ‘melting faster than ice’ in this summer heat

title: Watch: Iran deal talks advance as Trump signals major breakthrough | Above the Fold | 25.05.2026
content: From crucial U.S.-Iran talks and another sharp fuel price hike to the Padma Awards 2026, the Thoothukudi sexual assault case verdict - here are the top stories of the day.

title: India-China issues, impact of global conflicts, and the role of middle powers in spotlight at Huddle 2026
content: The theme of this year’s Huddle set to be held in Bengaluru on June 5 and 6 is ‘The World in Transition’; senior diplomats and experts on global affairs will discuss possible solutions to conflicts in West Asia and Europe

title:
MSME Growth Summit 2026: A.P. govt. forges 
key tie-ups to boost MSMEs, startups
content: Stone laid for 38 MSME parks; Rural Smart Village Centres to function as incubation and innovation centres for rural entrepreneurs; ₹20-crore seed fund programme announced for deep-tech startups

title: SC asks Delhi Government to come up with proposal for selection panel for DERC appointments
content: A dysfunctional Delhi Electricity Regulatory Commission would result in the piling up of petitions and applications that seek redress in electricity and energy-related issues in the national capital, the petition says

title: Petrol price crosses ₹110 per litre in Bengaluru, fourth hike in 10 days
content: The KSTOA says the continuous rise in fuel prices is affecting goods transport, cabs, buses, and even agriculture, with a cascading effect on commodity prices

title: PNG-migrated consumers can opt for transfer vouchers to restore LPG connection in future
content: The Union Petroleum Ministry held the provision would be particularly beneficial for transferable employees, migrant households, tenants and students, among others.

title: 87% winners in Assam polls got votes above 50%, shows ADR study
content: Sixteen (76%) out of 21 winners with declared criminal cases have won with a vote share of 50% and above and 94 (90%) out of 105 winners with clean backgrounds have won with a similar vote share

title: Fuel price hike increases cultivation costs, burdens farmers: Ponnam Prabhakar
content: BJP’s ‘Rythu Gosa – BJP Bharosa’ yatra a political drama amid pressing issues faced by farmers and common man, says Ponnam Prabhakar

title: Telangana CM expresses concern over Centre not procuring food grains at MSP
content: Families affected by the Musi rejuvenation project would be provided houses in the same vicinity, says Telangana CM Revanth Reddy

title: Naidu calls for blue economy vision plan for Andhra Pradesh coastline
content: Chief Minister directs officials to prepare a comprehensive strategy focusing on sustainable coastal development, marine resource utilisation, aquaculture value addition and ecological protection

title: Technical glitches in CBSE portal leave students seeking re-evaluation stranded
content: Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan has sought a detailed report from CBSE, deputed technical team from IIT Madras and IIT Kanpur to assist the board in solving tech issues

title: Krishna district faces record heat as temperatures hit 48.1°C
content: The present situation is arising due to a combination of factors, including urbanisation, development of heat domes, and other weather systems, says an expert

title: Belagavi may host Monsoon Session this time
content: The Chief Minister is yet to publicly announce his agreement to a suggestion made Legislative Council Chairman Basavaraj Horatti, according to senior officer

title: A.P. government launches MSME parks initiative to boost rural employment, development
content: The primary objective of the scheme is to create employment opportunities for local youth within the vicinity of their own villages, says Minority Welfare Minister while laying foundation stone for a park at Yalluru

title: Fuel price hike adds ₹40 crore burden on Karnataka RTCs in 10 days
content: Petrol and diesel prices were hiked for the fourth time in ten days on Monday, with fuel rates increasing by around ₹2.7 to ₹2.8 per litre across variants

title: ECI organises five-day workshop on electoral processes
content: The workshop aims at strengthening institutional preparedness in poll activities and supporting electoral management bodies in institutionalising structured electoral risk management frameworks, policies and operational systems

title: MLA Sridhar Reddy’s daughter enters politics, vows TDP win in Nellore
content: At her first press conference, Kotamreddy Sai Vaishnavi steps forward for the TDP in Nellore Rural, where her ailing father is the MLA and her uncle now runs party affairs

title: Annual Fruit Show to begin in Udhagamandalam on May 27
content: The 66th edition of the Fruit Show will be the last event of the festival this year, with the season officially closing on May 30

title: Twisha Sharma case: Giribala Singh given two days to file reply; CBI to take over case
content: Police rule out allegations of tampering with the CCTV camera footage from Giribala Singh’s house, where Twisha Sharma was found dead on the night of May 12.

title: Monsoon onset likely to be more frequent in May
content: An analysis of southwest monsoon onset data from 1901 to 2025 reveals that the monsoon has predominantly advanced to May in recent decades due to various factors, including global warming

title: No major headway in Muvattupuzha robbery case
content: The police have zeroed in on the maid, a native of Tamil Nadu hired nearly six months ago, as the prime suspect

title: Madhu lynching case: Kerala HC enhances sentence of 12 accused to life imprisonment, acquits one
content: Madhu’s mother and the Kerala government had filed appeals seeking enhanced punishment for the accused. The mentally challenged tribal youth was killed following the allegation that he had stolen rice from a grocery shop in February 2018

title: Delhi court convicts Alka Lamba in Jantar Mantar protest case
content: The Congress leader was booked after she and several others staged a demonstration at Jantar Mantar ahead of the 2024 Lok Sabha election, demanding the implementation of women’s reservation in Parliament

title: Eight years on, Madhu’s lynching still pricks Kerala’s conscience
content: The 27-year-old tribal man was accused of stealing food and lynched to death by a mob in Attappady on February 22, 2018. Post-mortem examination found extensive trauma, including fatal injuries to the back of the head, fractured ribs and 42 wounds on his body

title: BJP hits out at Rahul Gandhi over fuel price comments amid global tensions
content: Responding to Mr. Gandhi and Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge who commented on the latest increase in prices of petrol and diesel, BJP spokesperson Gaurav Bhatia says every decision taken by the government related to fuel prices would be in the public interest

title: P&T apartment residents to lay indefinite siege to GCDA office from May 26
content: Residents have been protesting the persistent leakage since 78 families moved into the apartments built by GCDA in January 2024

title: Fuel Prices Are Up, But It Still Won't Cover Oil Firms' Losses. Here's Why
content: Petroleum Minister Hardeep Singh Puri had said OMC under-recoveries were around Rs 1,000 crore a day.

title: "Alliance With Congress Will Insult Punjab Mandate": Bhagwant Mann To NDTV
content: The BJP and the Congress will barely cross the threshold of 10 seats in the 2027 assembly elections in Punjab, Bhagwant Mann predicted today.

title: "Our Fight Is For Survival": Delhi Gymkhana Club Staff After Eviction Order
content: Despite the reassurances, several employees noted that no written communication guaranteeing job security had been issued yet.

title: In Numbers: How Middle East War Led To Hike In Cooking, Commuting Costs
content: Even before the latest fuel hikes, kitchen items had already started to become costlier following the escalation in the Middle East war.

title: "Open Sale Of Drugs In Punjab Has Stopped": Bhagwant Mann To NDTV
content: Bhagwant Mann said the open sale of drugs in Punjab has completely stopped following the launch of the 'War on Drugs' campaign, and only cross-border covert operations exist now.

title: 'Divorced Daughter Better Than Dead': Top Law Officer In Twisha Sharma Case
content: Twisha Sharma's husband, Samarth Singh, who went missing right after his wife's death, surrendered before the cops after 10 days.

title: India Holds Crude Oil Reserves For 78 Days: Sources
content: The government, said sources, maintains that oil is available in sufficient quantities at petrol pumps.

title: Punjab Remote-Controlled By Arvind Kejriwal? What Bhagwant Mann Said
content: The AAP is a national party and Kejriwal has never interfered in his government's decision-making, Mann told NDTV's Rahul Kanwal at Nava Punjab summit today.

title: NDTV Nava Punjab Summit Live Updates: "AAP A National Party": Bhagwant Mann On 'Arvind Kejriwal Super CM' Charges
content: NDTV Nava Punjab Summit Live: Punjab Chief Minister Bhagwant Singh Mann, Chief Secretary KAP Sinha and DGP Gaurav Yadav are among the key figures speaking at the NDTV Nava Punjab Summit being held in Chandigarh today.

title: Manipur Chief Minister Flags Off 447 New Police Vehicles To Boost Security
content: The development comes at a time whne is Manipur is facing trouble in the hill region between the Kuki and Naga communities.

title: Delhi Issues Bakrid Advisory, Bans Cow, Camel Sacrifice In Public Places
content: The advisory, issued by Delhi Development minister Kapil Mishra, also states that animal sacrifice at public places such as roads, streets and open areas will not be permitted under any circumstances.

title: Anti-Terror Agency Conducts Raids Across Kashmir Targeting Jamaat-e-Islami
content: NIA teams, assisted by the police and the CRPF, carried out simultaneous searches in Srinagar and South Kashmir districts.

title: 'Trump Called India A Hellhole,' Reporter Tells Marco Rubio. His Response
content: The exchange referred to an incident in April when US President Trump shared on social media a derogatory post by a podcaster that described India as a "hellhole".

title: Another Setback For AIADMK, Three MLAs Cross Over To Vijay's TVK
content: The MLAs met Tamil Nadu minister Aadhav Arjuna, who is also a key aide of Vijay, sources said. Accompanied by Arjuna, the MLAs then went to meet the Speaker and handed their resignation, which got accepted.

title: "Want AIIMS Anywhere In Kerala, No Condition": State Health Minister
content: K Muraleedharan said even districts like Palakkad would be acceptable if the Centre considers them suitable for the premier medical institute.

title: Explained: What Needs To Be Agreed To End Iran War?
content: Iran's foreign ministry spokesperson said on Monday that conclusions have been reached on many topics discussed in a potential 14-point memorandum of understanding, but this does not mean a deal to end the Middle East war is imminent.

title: 50,000 Evacuated As Toxic Chemical Tank Sparks "Catastrophic Explosion" Fear
content: A storage tank at the GKN Aerospace facility contains about 7,000 gallons of a chemical called methyl methacrylate.

title: Mid-Air Scare For 300 Tourists As Technical Issue Halts Gulmarg Gondola
content: The cable car service, popularly known as Gondola, came to a sudden halt due to a technical fault, trapping tourists inside multiple cabins for a brief period and causing panic among visitors.

title: Major blow to AIADMK: 3 MLAs from Shanmugam–Velumani faction resign
content:
AIADMK general secretary Edappadi K Palaniswami

CHENNAI; MLAs S Jayakumar (Perundurai), Maragatham Kumaravel (Maduranthakam -sc) and P Sathyabama (Dharapuram - SC) on Monday submitted their resignation to Speaker J C D Prabhakar. They were among the 25 MLAs from C Ve Shanmugam- S P Velumani camp who voted in favour of Tamiliga Vettri Kahagam govt in the vote of confidence. Sources in the AIADMK said that the three MLAs decided to resign following a promise from TVK Chief Coordinator and Minister K A Sengottaiyan that they would be accommodated and fielded in their respective constituencies in the upcoming by-elections. Meanwhile, a couple of MLA from the rebel camp called on AIADMK general secretary Edappadi K Palaniswami as part of the ongoing reconciliation drive. Affirming this shift toward reunification, MLAs P Haribaskar (Anthiyur), S Mohan (Panruti) and S M Sugumar (Arcot) formally called on EPS at his Greenways Road residence

End of Article
